campaign.name: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club

npc.label: Flizzlebin [5.5e]
npc.name: Flizzlebin [5.5e]

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: Flizzlebin has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Ventriloquist
trait.text: Flizzlebin can project his voice from anywhere within 1 mile of himself.

action.name: Dazzling Confetti
action.text: Melee or Ranged Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Radiant damage.

action.name: Vanishing Trick
action.text: Flizzlebin can project his voice from anywhere within 1 mile of himself.

reaction.name: Distracting Glitter
reaction.text: Trigger: Flizzlebin is hit by an attack roll. Response: Flizzlebin adds 2 to his AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

npc.text: A trickster with a penchant for games, Flizzlebin tempts novice adventurers into dungeons to test their skills. The so-called "Vanishing Gnome's" favorite game involves hiding within dangerous dungeons and rewarding those who find him with priceless treasures.

note.name: Read
note.text:
This chamber has five stone doors, each bearing a different image and inscription.
The torches in the chamber flicker as a faint, disembodied voice calls to you. "I'm pleased you have accepted my quest," says Flizzlebin. "I am here, but I am not here. Find me, and I shall reward you greatly. Be forewarned! You are not the only creatures who seek me in this accursed place." A monstrous roar echoes throughout the dungeon.

note.name: Info
note.text: Flizzlebin speaks to the characters from the Shadow Dungeon (detailed later in this adventure). The gnome magically contacts the characters as they explore the dungeon, but he doesn't say anything else to characters in this area unless you decide otherwise. Flizzlebin has discovered that his communications have attracted the spawn of Demogorgon (see area D3), and he doesn't want to further draw its ire.

note.name: Doors
note.text:
Each of the five doors is adorned with a relief sculpture and an inscription as follows:
● Door to D2 This door bears the image of a fuming urn. The inscription reads "Wasteland of Filth." ● Door to D3 This door bears a relief of a ferocious, many-horned minotaur above a stone maze. The inscription reads "Labyrinth of Doom." ● Door to D4 In this door's relief, three mischievous creatures with pointed ears and sharp teeth surround an anvil. The inscription reads, "Forge of Gibbletrax." ● Door to D5 A spiderweb is etched into this door. The inscription reads "Den of Itsy." ● Door to D6 Etched into this door is the image of a scowling, bipedal hyenoid warrior. The door's inscription reads "Altar of Yeenoghu."

note.name: Read
note.text: A putrid stench hangs in this room. Gnawed bones, rotten food, and ancient junk litter the stone floor.

note.name: Info
note.text: This room holds refuse discarded by the dungeon's denizens.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: As a Search action, a character can sift through the trash and make a DC 12 Wisdom (Perception) check. The first character to succeed on the check unearths five ivory urns. Each urn is worth 10 GP. Four of the urns are empty, but one contains a Potion of Healing and a tiny pewter statue of a two-headed demon worth 90 GP. A character who inspects the statue and succeeds on a DC 13 Intelligence (Religion) check recognizes the statue as an idol of the demon lord Demogorgon.

item.name: Urn
item.text: Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

item.name: Potion of Healing [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Common
This potion is a magic item. As a Bonus Action, you can drink it or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of yourself. The creature that drinks the magical red fluid in this vial regains 2d4 + 2 Hit Points.
Source: Player's Handbook 2024 p. 228

item.name: Tiny Two Headed Demon Statue
item.text: Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

trait.name: Baleful Presence
trait.text: Sources of light in a 60-foot Emanation originating from the spawn flicker wildly. Nonmagical sources of Bright Light in that area instead shed Dim Light.

trait.name: Regeneration
trait.text: The spawn regains 5 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

trait.name: Running Leap
trait.text: With a 10-foot running start, the spawn can Long Jump up to 30 feet.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The spawn makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13). While Grappled, the target has the Blinded condition.

note.name: Read
note.text: The torches along the walls of this maze begin to flicker, and a familiar disembodied voice says, "Many adventurers have met their demise in these corridors. Beware the fearsome minotaur!"

note.name: Info
note.text: Until recently, a bloodthirsty minotaur ruled this trap-filled labyrinth. However, an even more vicious creature—the spawn of Demogorgon, a demon lord—escaped the Vale of Shadows and slew the minotaur. The spawn is devouring the minotaur's corpse in the middle of the labyrinth.

note.name: Spear Traps
note.text:
The labyrinth contains three spear traps triggered by pressure plates hidden in the floor, as denoted with a "T" on the Dungeon of Shadows map. Cobwebs and dust obscure the holes that house these spears.
Each spear trap has the following rules:
Detect and Disarm. As a Search action, a character can examine the walls and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check, detecting the holes on a successful check. Alternatively, a character can take the Study action to inspect the floor and make a DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check, detecting the pressure plate on a successful check.
Triggering the Trap. When a creature moves onto a trap's pressure plate for the first time on a turn, spears thrust out from the walls. Each creature on the pressure plate must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity Saving Throw or be struck by a spear, taking 1d6 Piercing damage. The spears then retract, and the trap resets immediately.

note.name: Terror in the Maze
note.text:
When the characters reach the center of the maze, read or paraphrase the following text:
"The torches in this bloodstained corridor sputter erratically. A thin, sinewy creature with slimy, gray skin and elongated limbs crouches over a slain minotaur. The corpse squelches and snaps unsettlingly as the creature gorges on it. A small chest lies beside the mangled minotaur."
The Demogorgon Spawn continues devouring the minotaur for 1 minute or until it detects the characters. The spawn is Hostile toward all other creatures.
To sneak past the spawn while it feeds, a character must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity (Stealth) check. On a failed check, or if the characters otherwise disturb the spawn, it turns toward the adventurers. When that happens, read or paraphrase the following text:
"The creature turns toward you, and its faceless head peels back to reveal five toothed, fleshy petals around a gaping mouth. It screeches and rushes toward you.
Roll Initiative!"
The Demogorgon spawn fights until it is reduced to 25 Hit Points or fewer, at which point it retreats through the secret door to area S3 of the Shadow Dungeon (detailed later in this adventure). If the characters manage to defeat the Demogorgon spawn here, the adventure doesn't end; the characters still must find Flizzlebin to complete his quest.
If the characters don't disturb the spawn and aren't detected, the creature finishes its meal and then relocates to area S4 in the Shadow Dungeon.

note.name: Secret Door
note.text: A secret door (see "Dungeon Features") is set into the wall in the southeast corner of the labyrinth. It leads to area S3. Characters who witnessed the Demogorgon spawn use this secret door automatically succeed on checks to find the door.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: The chest in the middle of the labyrinth contains a Wand of Secrets and 25 GP.

item.name: Wand of Secrets [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Uncommon
This wand has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge, and if a secret door or trap is within 60 feet of you, the wand pulses and points at the one nearest to you.
Source: Dungeon Master's Guide 2024 p. 322

item.name: Gold Piece (GP) [5.5e]
item.text:
Characters often find coins on their adventures and can spend those coins in shops, inns, and other businesses. Coins come in different denominations based on the relative worth of their material. The Coin Values table lists coins and how much they're worth relative to the Gold Piece, which is the game's main coin. For example, 100 Copper Pieces are worth 1 Gold Piece. A coin weighs about a third of an ounce, so fifty coins weigh a pound.
Coin | Value in GP Copper Piece (CP) | 1/100 Silver Piece (SP) | 1/10 Electrum Piece (EP) | 1/2 Gold Piece (GP) | 1 Platinum Piece (PP) | 10
Source: Player's Handbook 2024 p. 213

note.name: Read
note.text:
Clanging hammers ring out in this sweltering chamber. Three goblin smiths cluster around a glowing forge in the center of the room. The goblins sing in scratchy tones as they work:
"Deep within the Dungeon of Shadows,
We strengthen steel to slay the beasts.
Upside down in the Vale of Shadows,
Brave heroes delve to keep us free."

note.name: Info
note.text:
Blessed by a long-forgotten god, the Forge of Gibbletrax is a magic forge stewarded by three Goblin Warriors named Ble, Gib, and Trax. These smiths craft gear to defend against the dungeon's threats.
The goblins are initially Friendly toward the characters but defend themselves if necessary. If the characters approach the goblins peacefully, the goblins greet the characters warmly and ask what brings them to the dungeon.
Ble, Glib, and Trax usually keep to themselves, but they recently clashed with the gnolls in area D6, whose profane rituals threaten the sanctity of the forge. If the characters agree to end the gnolls' foul rites, the goblins vow to imbue the characters' weapons with the forge's magic.

note.name: Forge of Gibbletrax
note.text:
The goblins' magical forge can temporarily infuse weapons and ammunition with immense heat. As a Bonus Action, a character can dip one weapon or piece of ammunition into the forge, coating it in a thin layer of molten ore. A creature that takes damage from the ore-coated weapon or ammunition takes an extra 1d6 Fire damage. Once coated, the weapon retains its heat for 10 minutes or until its damage is dealt, whichever comes first.
A character can benefit from the Forge of Gibbletrax only once, unless the goblins permit them to do so again. If all three goblins are slain or the forge is removed from this area, its magic ceases to function.

note.name: You can do this!
note.text: Sure, these smiths could have been dwarves, but that would've been BORING. Anyone can be a smith, just like anyone can be a Dungeon Master.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 359, Player's Handbook 2024 p. 351

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Web Walker
trait.text: The spider ignores movement restrictions caused by webs, and it knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.

action.name: Web (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature the spider can see within 60 feet. Failure: The target has the Restrained condition until the web is destroyed (AC 10; HP 5; Vulnerability to Fire damage; Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage).

note.name: Read
note.text:
Thick, sticky webs cover this once-regal chamber. Skeletons encased in cocoons hang from webs on the ceiling as grim warnings to all who enter.
A cobwebbed statue of an eight-foot-tall, armor-clad knight presides over the chamber. Before it is a stone pedestal, atop which rests a shimmering crystal spider roughly five feet in diameter. A fiery greatsword burns within the crystal spider's body, casting a warm, orange glow on the tangled nest.

note.name: DM Secret!
note.text: Don't reveal the Giant Spider yet. This monster is hiding.

note.name: Info
note.text: This room is the nest of Itsy, a Giant Spider that preys on any who enter its lair. The Hostile spider clings to the ceiling, guarding the secret door and treasure in this room (detailed below). If a character opens the secret door or disturbs the crystal spider, Itsy begins combat.

note.name: Crystal Spider
note.text: The crystal spider is a Medium object with AC 13, HP 50, Resistance to Piercing and Slashing damage, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. Destroying the crystal spider frees the treasure inside (detailed below).

note.name: Secret Door
note.text: Set into the east wall of this room is a secret door (see "Dungeon Features") that connects to area S5.

note.name: Statue
note.text:
The statue depicts a knight in a gallant pose. As a Study action, a character can examine the statue and make a DC 12 Intelligence (Investigation) check. On a successful check, the character realizes the statue's base is unstable; a significant push could topple the statue.
As an action, a character within 5 feet of the statue can make a DC 10 Strength (Athletics) check to topple the statue into a space within 5 feet of it, doing so on a successful check. When the statue topples, each creature beneath it takes 4d6 Bludgeoning damage and has the Prone condition. The statue crumbles on impact, revealing a treasure hidden inside it (see "Treasure" below).

note.name: Webs
note.text:
The web-filled areas are Difficult Terrain.
A creature that enters a web-filled area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 12 Dexterity Saving Throw or have the Restrained condition. As an action, a creature Restrained by the webs can try to escape from them, doing so with a successful DC 12 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check.
Each 5-foot Cube of webs has AC 10; HP 7; Vulnerability to Fire damage; and Immunity to Piercing, Poison, and Psychic damage.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: Inside the statue is a heart-shaped emerald worth 30 GP. The sword encased in the crystal spider is a Flame Tongue (Greatsword).

item.name: Flame Tongue Greatsword [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Rare (requires Attunement)
While holding this magic weapon, you can take a Bonus Action and use a command word to cause flames to engulf the damage-dealing part of the weapon. These flames shed Bright Light in a 40-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 40 feet. While the weapon is ablaze, it deals an extra 2d6 Fire damage on a hit. The flames last until you take a Bonus Action to issue the command again or until you drop, stow, or sheathe the weapon.
Graze (Mastery). If your attack roll with this weapon misses a creature, you can deal damage to that creature equal to the ability modifier you used to make the attack roll. This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier.
Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it's a Melee weapon and your Strength score isn't at least 13 or if it's a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score isn't at least 13.
Two-Handed. A Two-Handed weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Source: Dungeon Master's Guide 2024 p. 263

action.name: Rampage (1/Day) (bonus action)

note.name: Read
note.text: In this square chamber, three hyenoid warriors kneel before an altar bearing a wicked-looking flail. Two of the creatures cackle maniacally as the third—the mangiest of the three who's nearly devoid of hair—chants, "Yeenoghu! Hear our laughter and let there be bloodshed and butchery!" The flail glows with sickly light.

note.name: Info
note.text: These three Gnoll Warriors worship Yeenoghu, a merciless demon lord who delights in slaughter. The Hostile gnolls fight all other creatures on sight.

note.name: Completing the Ritual
note.text:
If the characters don't interrupt the gnolls, they continue their ritual. After 1 minute, booming laughter erupts from the altar. Any creatures other than the gnolls in this area must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 1d8 Thunder damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
After the ritual, the gnolls leave to battle the goblins in area D4. If the characters don't intervene, the gnolls overpower the goblins, and the Forge of Gibbletrax loses its magic forever.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: The flail on the altar is a +1 Flail.

item.name: Flail +1 [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Uncommon
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Sap (Mastery). If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.
Source: Dungeon Master's Guide 2024 p. 324

monster.label: Shadow [5.5e]
monster.name: Shadow [5.5e]
monster.conditionImmune: exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 272

trait.name: Amorphous
trait.text: The shadow can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

trait.name: Sunlight Weakness
trait.text: While in sunlight, the shadow has Disadvantage on D20 Tests.

action.name: Draining Swipe
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) Necrotic damage, and the target's Strength score decreases by 1d4. The target dies if this reduces that score to 0. If a Humanoid is slain by this attack, a Shadow rises from the corpse 1d4 hours later.

action.name: Shadow Stealth (bonus action)
action.text: While in Dim Light or Darkness, the shadow takes the Hide action.

note.name: Read
note.text: This eerie chamber has five stone doors, each bearing a vile image and inscription. A dead dwarf is slumped in a shadowy corner. The corpse clutches a torn sheet of parchment in its stiff hand. Near the dwarf, a stone staircase blocked by rubble ascends into darkness.

note.name: Info
note.text:
This room mirrors the Chamber of Choice (area D1). The corpse is that of a dwarf explorer who perished four days ago while searching for Flizzlebin. The dwarf was killed by an Undead shadow.
The dwarf's body is unnaturally thin, and its clothes look too big. A character can inspect the corpse and make a DC 12 Wisdom (Medicine) check. On a successful check, the character discerns the dwarf's life was sapped by a supernatural force.
The dwarf clutches a note. If the characters remove it from the corpse, show the players the Dungeon Warning Handout. After the characters read the note, a Shadow rises from the dwarf's corpse and begins combat.

note.name: Doors
note.text:
Each of the five doors is adorned with a relief sculpture and an inscription as follows:
● Door to S2 (Urn). This door bears the image of an urn wrapped in ink-black vines. The inscription reads "Wasteland of Filthy Vines." ● Door to S3 (Labyrinth). This door's relief depicts a fungal maze. The inscription reads, "Labyrinth of Gloom." ● Door to S4 (Spiked Anvil). In this door's relief, a shadowy figure looms over a spiked anvil. The inscription reads, "Forge of Annihilation." ● Door to S5 (Web). A black spiderweb is etched into this door. The inscription reads "Den of Bitsy." ● Door to S6 (Hand and Eye). A desiccated hand holding an eye decorates this door. Deep scratches make the inscription illegible.

note.name: Staircase
note.text: The staircase, blocked by rubble, leads to the Vale of Shadows. That realm and its contents are beyond the scope of this adventure, but you can flesh them out as you see fit. Otherwise, the staircase is impassible.

item.name: Dwarf Note
item.text:
If another should find this note, take heed. This is not the realm you know.
DO NOT be fooled by the familiar corridors of this forsaken place, where shadows strangle their masters.
Evil dwells here. I have lost the way. My companions are dead. I fear I will join them soon.
Leave this place at ONCE ... if you still can.
Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

note.name: Read
note.text: Slithering black vines squirm along the floor of this trash-filled room.

note.name: Info
note.text: This room mirrors the Wasteland of Filth (area D2). The floor is Difficult Terrain.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: The first character to sift through the trash and succeed on a DC 12 Wisdom (Perception) check unearths five cracked urns hewn from black granite. The urns are worthless. Four of the urns are empty, but one contains a Potion of Healing and a glass eye with an emerald pupil worth 10 GP.

item.name: Potion of Healing [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Common
This potion is a magic item. As a Bonus Action, you can drink it or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of yourself. The creature that drinks the magical red fluid in this vial regains 2d4 + 2 Hit Points.
Source: Player's Handbook 2024 p. 228

item.name: Glass Eye
item.text: Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

trait.name: Treasure
trait.text: Any. The monster's treasure hoard can include monetary treasure and any kinds of magic items.

trait.name: Sun Sickness
trait.text: While in sunlight, the myconid has Disadvantage on D20 Tests. The myconid dies if it spends more than 1 hour in sunlight.

action.name: Slam
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d8) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.

action.name: Pacifying Spores (1/Day)
action.text: Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature the myconid can see within 10 feet. Failure: The target has the Stunned condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

action.name: Rapport Spores
action.text: The myconid expels spores in a 30-foot Emanation originating from itself. Creatures in that area with an Intelligence score of 2 or higher that aren't Constructs, Elementals, or Undead gain telepathy with a range of 30 feet for 1 hour.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 223

trait.name: Treasure
trait.text: Any. The monster's treasure hoard can include monetary treasure and any kinds of magic items.

trait.name: Sun Sickness
trait.text: While in sunlight, the myconid has Disadvantage on D20 Tests. The myconid dies if it spends more than 1 hour in sunlight.

action.name: Slam
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 4 (1d8) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 (1d6) Poison damage.

action.name: Pacifying Spores (1/Day)
action.text: Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature the myconid can see within 10 feet. Failure: The target has the Stunned condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

action.name: Rapport Spores
action.text: The myconid expels spores in a 30-foot Emanation originating from itself. Creatures in that area with an Intelligence score of 2 or higher that aren't Constructs, Elementals, or Undead gain telepathy with a range of 30 feet for 1 hour.

note.name: Read
note.text: Clouds of spores drift throughout this stone labyrinth. The maze is overgrown with fungi and foot-tall mushrooms that sprout from the walls and floor.

note.name: Info
note.text:
This stone maze mirrors the Labyrinth of Doom (area D3). The maze is home to toxic plants and four Shrieker Fungi. Two Hostile Myconid Adults also roam the maze, fighting any non-Plants they encounter.
If the shriekers sound off, the myconids investigate the disturbance. The shriekers don't shriek if the myconids come within 30 feet of them.

note.name: Secret Door
note.text: A secret door (see "Shadow Dungeon Features") is set into the wall near the southeast corner of the maze. It leads to area D3.

note.name: Spore Traps
note.text:
The labyrinth contains three spore traps—swollen plant bulbs that exhale puffs of toxic spores—triggered by stepping on pressure plates of inflated mushrooms. These traps are marked on Dungeon of Shadows map with a "T." The fungus-infested walls obscure the bulbs that spray these spores.
Each spore trap has the following rules:
Detect and Disarm. As a Study action, a character can examine the walls and make a DC 15 Intelligence (Nature) check, identifying the toxic bulbs on a successful check. Alternatively, a character can take the Study action to inspect the floor and make a DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check, identifying the fungal pressure plate on a successful check. Trimming the stems that connect the bulbs to the pressure plate prevents the trap from triggering.
Triggering the Trap. When a creature moves onto a pressure plate for the first time on a turn, the bulbs spray toxic spores that fill a 5-foot Cube centered on the pressure plate. Each creature in the Cube must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution Saving Throw or have the Poisoned condition for 1 hour. The trap resets immediately.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: A dilapidated chest in the middle of the labyrinth contains a broken wand and 25 GP. The wand is useless.

item.name: Broken Wand
item.text: Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

item.name: Gold Piece (GP) [5.5e]
item.text:
Characters often find coins on their adventures and can spend those coins in shops, inns, and other businesses. Coins come in different denominations based on the relative worth of their material. The Coin Values table lists coins and how much they're worth relative to the Gold Piece, which is the game's main coin. For example, 100 Copper Pieces are worth 1 Gold Piece. A coin weighs about a third of an ounce, so fifty coins weigh a pound.
Coin | Value in GP Copper Piece (CP) | 1/100 Silver Piece (SP) | 1/10 Electrum Piece (EP) | 1/2 Gold Piece (GP) | 1 Platinum Piece (PP) | 10
Source: Player's Handbook 2024 p. 213

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

trait.name: Baleful Presence
trait.text: Sources of light in a 60-foot Emanation originating from the spawn flicker wildly. Nonmagical sources of Bright Light in that area instead shed Dim Light.

trait.name: Regeneration
trait.text: The spawn regains 5 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

trait.name: Running Leap
trait.text: With a 10-foot running start, the spawn can Long Jump up to 30 feet.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The spawn makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13). While Grappled, the target has the Blinded condition.

note.name: Read
note.text: An unholy forge dominates this grim chamber. Caustic fumes waft from its icy hearth, which swirls with a faintly glowing, pale-green slurry. Skulls bob along the liquid's surface.

note.name: Info
note.text:
This room mirrors the Forge of Gibbletrax (area D4). If the Demogorgon spawn survived its previous encounter with the characters (see area D3), it lurks here. Read or paraphrase the following text:
"A familiar, horrible creature—the same creature you encountered in the labyrinth above—rises from the shadow of the forge. It unfurls its faceless head once more and chitters in anticipation.
Roll Initiative!"
The Demogorgon Spawn fights to the death.

note.name: Forge of Annihilation
note.text:
Unlike the goblins' blessed forge in the dungeon above, this forge is a cursed apparatus capable only of destruction. The Forge of Annihilation causes living creatures to wither and die. As a Study action, a character can inspect the forge and make a DC 13 Intelligence (Arcana) check. On a successful check, the character identifies the eldritch hazard and the threat it poses.
A creature that enters the forge for the first time or starts its turn there must make a DC 15 Constitution Saving Throw, taking 4d8 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 359, Player's Handbook 2024 p. 351

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Web Walker
trait.text: The spider ignores movement restrictions caused by webs, and it knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.

action.name: Web (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 13, one creature the spider can see within 60 feet. Failure: The target has the Restrained condition until the web is destroyed (AC 10; HP 5; Vulnerability to Fire damage; Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage).

note.name: Read
note.text:
A statue of an eight-foot-tall knight with a horned, crimson helm stands in this chamber, which is covered in thick, black webs. Atop an altar in the center of the room, a massive spider with dripping fangs wraps a dead elf in webs.
Humanoid-shaped cocoons—the arachnid's ghastly larder—hang throughout the chamber.

note.name: Info
note.text: This room mirrors the Den of Itsy (area D5). It belongs to Bitsy, an equally territorial Giant Spider. Bitsy fights the characters on sight.

note.name: Secret Door
note.text: Set into the east wall in this room is a secret door (see "Shadow Dungeon Features") that connects to area D5.

note.name: Statue
note.text:
The statue depicts a knight with a menacing helm. As a Study action, a character can examine the statue and make a DC 12 Intelligence (Investigation) check. On a successful check, the character realizes the statue's base is unstable; a significant push could topple the statue.
As an action, a character within 5 feet of the statue can make a DC 10 Strength (Athletics) check to topple the statue into a space within 5 feet of it, doing so on a successful check. When the statue topples, each creature beneath it takes 4d6 Bludgeoning damage and has the Prone condition. The statue crumbles on impact, revealing treasure within (see "Treasure" below), but its helm remains intact.

note.name: Webs
note.text:
The web-filled areas are Difficult Terrain.
A creature that enters a web-filled area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 12 Dexterity Saving Throw or have the Restrained condition. As an action, a creature Restrained by the webs can try to escape from them, doing so with a successful DC 12 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check.
Each 5-foot Cube of webs has AC 10; HP 7; Vulnerability to Fire damage; and Immunity to Piercing, Poison, and Psychic damage.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: The statue's helmet is a crimson Dread Helm that resembles the head of a horned devil. Inside the statue is a heart-shaped black tourmaline worth 30 GP.

item.name: Dread Helm [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Common
While you're wearing this fearsome steel helm, your eyes glow red and the rest of your face is hidden in shadow.
Source: Dungeon Master's Guide 2024 p. 254

item.name: Tourmaline [5.5e]
item.text:
A pale green, blue, brown, or red gemstone.
Source: Dungeon Master's Guide 2024 p. 214

note.name: Read
note.text: Low fog pervades this eerily quiet chamber. A stone altar rises from the center of the floor. Atop the altar, a dismembered hand grasps an hourglass half-filled with black sand and adorned with bone spindles. The sand defies gravity, flowing to the top bulb.

note.name: Info
note.text:
This room mirrors the Altar of Yeenoghu (area D6), but this altar honors evil of a viler caliber.
The first character to inspect the hourglass hears the sound of a ticking clock, followed by a baleful chime. The hourglass then cracks, and the sand spills out, revealing a mummified eye within the lower bulb. No other character hears the sound.
Afterward, a character can examine the altar's contents and make a DC 12 Intelligence (Religion) check. On a successful check, the character recognizes the hand and the eye as the symbol of Vecna, a powerful lich feared throughout Greyhawkins. The hourglass is a ceremonial object used by members of Vecna's cult to symbolize the lich's dreaded—and inevitable—return.

item.name: Mummified Eye
item.text: Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

item.name: Cap of Vanishing [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Uncommon (requires Attunement)
This cap has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the cap, you can take a Magic action and expend 1 charge to give yourself the Invisible condition for 10 minutes. The effect ends early if the hat is removed, or immediately after you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell.
Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

note.name: Adventure Info
note.text:
The Vanishing Gnome is an adventure for Level 1 Characters.
This adventure is designed to fill one or two sessions of play.
If you aren't using the Map of Greyhawkins, it can take place in any shadowy dungeon.

note.name: Key Plot Points
note.text:
This information is key to the adventure's story:
Plot Point 1. Flizzlebin magically contacts the characters and challenges them to find him in the Dungeon of Shadows.
Plot Point 2. The characters navigate the Dungeon of Shadows in search of Flizzlebin, who calls out to them from his hiding place in a twisted reflection of the dungeon connected to a mysterious realm known as the Vale of Shadows.
Plot Point 3. A spawn of Demogorgon, a demon lord, has escaped the Vale of Shadows. The characters first encounter the creature in the Dungeon of Shadows, but it likely escapes.
Plot Point 4. After defeating the Demogorgon Spawn, the characters find Flizzlebin and earn the gnome's reward.

note.name: DM Preparation
note.text:
Before running the adventure, prepare as follows:
Step 1. Read the "Adventure Background" section.
Step 2. Read the "Hide-and-Seek" section and secretly note Flizzlebin's hiding spot.
Step 3. Familiarize yourself with the creatures in this adventure, which are as follows:
1 Demogorgon Spawn Flizzlebin 1 Giant Spider 3 Gnoll Warriors 2 Myconid Adults 3 Goblin Warriors 1 Shadow 4 Shrieker Fungi
Step 4. Familiarize yourself with the magic items in this adventure, which are as follows:
Cap of Vanishing +1 Flail Flame Tongue (Greatsword) 2 Potions of Healing Wand of Secrets

note.name: Eddy Note
note.text: Don't let anyone from Hawkins PD catch you with this. Officer Callahan still thinks I stole all those lawn gnomes that went missing a few years back. Consider this booklet evidence.

note.name: Player Assistance
note.text: You can simplify the DM's role by having the a player handle tasks typically assigned to the DM. Before play begins, see if a player would like to be the Rules Consultant. That player references the D&D Beyond Basic Rules as needed.

note.name: Adventure Background
note.text:
Flizzlebin, the Vanishing Gnome, tests the mettle of would-be heroes in dangerous games of hide-and-seek in dungeons throughout the land. Over the years, the elusive gnome has magically contacted countless travelers, but few have accepted his quests—and fewer have completed them. Successful seekers have emerged from these dungeons with priceless treasures and pockets brimming with gold.
Recently, Flizzlebin vanished to the Dungeon of Shadows, a maze that borders a gloomy, monster-infested realm. There, his calls to adventurers awakened a foul creature: a spawn of the demon lord Demogorgon! As Flizzlebin eagerly broadcasts his quest, the ravenous demon stalks the dungeon, complicating the gnome's game and endangering all who seek him.

note.name: Hide-and-Seek
note.text:
Flizzlebin hides in the Shadow Dungeon, a mirror of the Dungeon of Shadows. Roll on the Flizzlebin's Hiding Spot table to determine where the characters can find the gnome in the dungeon (detailed later in this adventure). Flizzlebin avoids harm in his hiding place by using his Cap of Vanishing.
Flizzlebin's Hiding Spot 1d6 | Location 1 | Chamber of Bad Choices (area S1) 2 | Wasteland of Filthy Vines (area S2) 3 | Labyrinth of Gloom (area S3) 4 | Forge of Annihilation (area S4) 5 | Den of Bitsy (area S5) 6 | Altar of Vecna (area S6)

note.name: Beginning the Adventure
note.text:
When the players are ready to begin the adventure, read or paraphrase the following text:
"You travel a well-trod road through craggy and forested country. As you pass a dense thicket, a voice calls out to you exuberantly.
"Well-met, adventurers! 'Tis I, Flizzlebin, the Vanishing Gnome, the illustrious vagabond, the emissary of evasion! I have sequestered myself deep within the fabled Dungeon of Shadows, where I wait with a magical reward for those who prove themselves worthy. Find me, and the treasure is yours. Travel east to find the dungeon's entrance. Beware! Horrid creatures lurk within the dungeon's darkest corners... and so do I."
The voice fades. Do you accept this quest?"
At your discretion, one or more of the characters might have heard of Flizzlebin from stories shared in taverns throughout the land. If the characters follow the gnome's instructions, they quickly arrive at the Dungeon of Shadows.

note.name: Eddy Note
note.text: I've run this adventure a bunch of times. Every once in a while, I wind up with a player who has played the damn thing already, and you will, too. My advice? Never run it the same way twice. Hide Flizzlebin somewhere else. Add a couple of Vine Blights to the Shadow Dungeon. That'll keep everyone on their toes and stop blabbermouths from spoiling the fun for the newbies—and themselves.

note.name: Dungeon of Shadows
note.text:
The Dungeon of Shadows is the bridge between Greyhawkins and the Vale of Shadows, a mirror world in a realm of shadow. Bloodthirsty gnolls, giant arachnids, deadly traps, and worse terrors await visitors to the dungeon.
A warped version of the dungeon is reflected within the Vale of Shadows. Flizzlebin hides within this shadow of the dungeon above, which is accessible only through portals hidden throughout the dungeon.

note.name: Arriving at the Dungeon
note.text:
When the characters arrive at the Dungeon of Shadows, read or paraphrase the following text:
"You reach the entrance to the Dungeon of Shadows. Black briars grow around the entrance's mouth, which resembles a howling stone face. Past the opening, rough-hewn stairs descend into darkness."
A character can inspect the entrance and make one of the following checks to recall lore about the dungeon and the gloomy realm it borders:
DC 12 Intelligence (History). Monsters infest this dungeon, which is suffused with shadowy magic. The dungeon was once a temple devoted to a long-forgotten god, and its reputation is as tenebrous as its entrance. Rumors speak of a second, more sinister dungeon connected to this one.
DC 12 Intelligence (Arcana). The dungeon is magically linked to the Vale of Shadows, a grim reflection of the natural world. Dreadful creatures and living shadows lurk in this dark and twisted reality; occasionally, some of these creatures escape the vale to plague the people of Greyhawkins.
The staircase descends 60 feet to area D1. When the characters enter the dungeon, use the Dungeon of Shadows Map to see where the characters begin in that area.

note.name: Eddy Note
note.text: Flizzlebin can speak to the characters whenever you want him to. If your players are really green, he can be the voice of the DM, A.K.A. the voice of reason. He might contact the characters to drop a hint, give words of encouragement... or warn them of CERTAIN DEATH.

note.name: DM Secret!
note.text: The map for this adventure depicts the Dungeon of Shadows and its reflection in the Vale of Shadows. Keep the Vale of Shadows hidden from your players until they enter it, as detailed later in this adventure.

note.name: Dungeon Features
note.text:
The dungeon has the following features:
Ceilings. The ceilings are 20 feet high in rooms and 10 feet high in passages.
Doors and Secret Doors. Doors are unlocked and made of basalt. Secret doors manifest as spidery, elastic fissures that blend in with their surroundings. As a Search action, a character can examine a wall that contains a secret door and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check, noticing it on a successful check. A secret door opens as easily as any normal door.
Lighting. Torches in iron wall sconces fill the dungeon with Bright Light.

note.name: Shadow Dungeon
note.text: Beneath the Dungeon of Shadows lies the Shadow Dungeon, a gloomy reflection of the dungeon that borders the Vale of Shadows. Though its chambers mirror the areas above, their contents differ, distorted by the eerie realm they border.

note.name: Finding Flizzlebin
note.text:
To find Flizzlebin, the characters must defeat the Demogorgon spawn and any Hostile creatures in Flizzlebin's hiding spot (see the "Hide-and-Seek" section earlier in this adventure). When both conditions are met, the gnome appears to the characters; proceed to this adventure's conclusion.
If the characters reach Flizzlebin's hiding spot before they vanquish the Demogorgon spawn, read or paraphrase the following text:
""Ah! You found my hiding spot! But your work isn't done yet," says a familiar voice. "To claim your prize, you must slay the horrid creature that hunts me." "
Flizzlebin doesn't bequeath his reward until the characters comply with his instructions. At your discretion, he might reveal the Demogorgon spawn's location—likely area S4.

note.name: Shadow Dungeon Features
note.text:
The Shadow Dungeon has the following features:
Ceilings. The ceilings are 20 feet high in rooms and 10 feet high in passages.
Lighting. The dungeon is unlit. Area descriptions assume the characters have a light source or other means of seeing in Darkness. Bare iron sconces line the walls.
Doors and Secret Doors. Doors are unlocked and made of basalt. Secret doors manifest as spidery, elastic fissures that blend in with their surroundings and exude faint rays of natural light when opened. As a Search action, a character can examine a wall that contains a secret door and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check, noticing it on a successful check.

note.name: Conclusion
note.text:
After the characters defeat the Demogorgon spawn and locate Flizzlebin, the gnome rewards them. Read or paraphrase the following:
"A bearded gnome in a fancy cap addresses you enthusiastically. "Bravery, majesty, and teamwork!" he exclaims. "How wonderful it was to guide you on your quest." He removes his cap.
"For your heroism, I bestow on you my magic cap. But know this: The real treasures were the bonds you formed along the way.
"Go forth! Many great adventures await you." "
For their efforts, Flizzlebin gives the characters his Cap of Vanishing. If one of the characters didn't find a magic item during the adventure, the gnome presents the cap to that character.
Flizzlebin is Friendly and willing to speak to the characters. During conversation, he fondly recalls the characters' deeds and commends them on their creativity, heroism, or any other qualities you deem appropriate. He then wishes them farewell and vanishes once more, disappearing to a faraway dungeon to challenge a new group of adventurers.

note.name: Character Advancement
note.text: After the characters receive Flizzlebin's reward, everyone in the party gains 1 level. Direct each player to record any treasure they found over the course of this adventure.

note.name: Eddy Note
note.text:
You can bring back Flizzlebin the next time you want to send the characters to a dungeon or reward a player with an item they've been pestering you about. Mike is the worst with this.
"Eddie! When are we going to level up?"
"Eddie! When do I get a Vorpal Sword?"
Obnoxious. Make him work for it.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 77

trait.name: Gear
trait.text: Club

trait.name: Treasure
trait.text: Individual. The monster doesn't have a treasure hoard, but it might keep monetary treasure.

trait.name: Size
trait.text: Small, Medium

trait.name: Training
trait.text: The commoner has proficiency in one skill of the DM's choice and has Advantage whenever it makes an ability check using that skill.

action.name: Club
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 5 ft. Hit: 2 (1d4) Bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: Rillt June makes two Hayfork attacks.

action.name: Hayfork
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: G'wan, Git! (1/Day)
action.text: Wisdome Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature Rilly June can see within 60 feet. Failure: 10 (3d6) Psychic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the end of its next turn. Success: Half damage only.

note.name: Read
note.text: A thin mist rises from a dirt tunnel. Writhing black vines cling to the earthen walls. A distant, bone-chilling howl echoes from the depths.

note.name: Info
note.text:
The howl originated in Gunthar's crypt (area T9), where the late vampire's monstrous hounds reside. A character who hears the howl and succeeds on a DC 13 Wisdom (Nature) check knows that no ordinary dog or wolf can produce such a sound.
The tunnel exits to a field on Rillard Family Farm.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 126

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The fungus makes two Rotting Touch attacks.

action.name: Rotting Touch
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 10 ft. Hit: 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 126

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The fungus makes two Rotting Touch attacks.

action.name: Rotting Touch
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +2, reach 10 ft. Hit: 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 44

action.name: Constricting Vine
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12). Until the grapple ends, the target takes 4 (1d8) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns, and the blight can't make Constricting Vine attacks.

action.name: Entangling Plants (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The blight casts the Entangle spell, using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12).

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 44

action.name: Constricting Vine
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12). Until the grapple ends, the target takes 4 (1d8) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns, and the blight can't make Constricting Vine attacks.

action.name: Entangling Plants (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The blight casts the Entangle spell, using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12).

note.name: Read
note.text: Two plants shaped like droplets with purple lattice-like branches sprout from the ground in this tunnel. Their tendrils snake toward the ceiling and intertwine with vines that cover every surface.

note.name: DM Secret!
note.text: Don't reveal the location of the Vine Blight Tanglers yet. These monsters are hiding. At the start of combat, any character who didn't notice these blights has Disadvantage on their Initiative roll.

note.name: Info
note.text: The purple plants are two Violet Fungi. Two Vine Blight Tanglers hide among the Gulthias roots. As a Search action, a character can survey the area and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check, detecting the blights on a successful check. The fungi and blights are Hostile toward all non-Plant creatures. If such a creature comes within 10 feet of the plants or otherwise disturbs them, they enter combat.

monster.label: Scarecrow [5.5e]
monster.name: Scarecrow [5.5e]
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, unconscious

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 269

action.name: Fearsome Claw
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Slashing damage, and the target has the Frightened condition until the end of the scarecrow's next turn.

action.name: Terrifying Glare
action.text: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 11, one creature the scarecrow can see within 30 feet. Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the end of the scarecrow's next turn. While Frightened, the target has the Paralyzed condition.

note.name: Read
note.text:
Tiny white spores drift through the air in this musty tunnel. Black vines creep up to the ceiling, where a shiny pendant hangs from a gnarled root, glinting innocently.
Scarecrows stand silently throughout the chamber. Each has a rotten pumpkin for a head.

note.name: DM Secret!
note.text: Don't reveal the Scarecrow yet. This monster is hiding. At the start of combat, any character who didn't notice the scarecrow has Disadvantage on their Initiative roll.

note.name: Info
note.text:
Though most of the scarecrows in this tunnel are inanimate, one of them is a Hostile Scarecrow. It remains motionless, hoping to ambush the characters. As a Study action, a character can inspect the scarecrows and make a DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check. On a successful check, the character recognizes the living scarecrow hiding among the inanimate ones.
If the characters disturb the pendant or the scarecrow, the scarecrow begins combat, surprising any characters who didn't identify it as a threat.

note.name: Stalking Scarecrow
note.text:
If the characters don't fight the scarecrow in this area, it follows them after they leave this tunnel. It strikes when the characters are most vulnerable, such as immediately after combat with the ankheg (see area T5).
At your discretion, if the characters later return to this area, one or more scarecrows might be missing, suggesting that others might be hunting them.

note.name: Toxic Spores
note.text: A cloud of toxic spores fills this area. When a creature enters the area, it must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition for 1 minute. A creature that fails the save repeats it at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. A creature that succeeds on its save is immune to the effect of these spores for 24 hours.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: The pendant that hangs from the ceiling is a Periapt of Health.

item.name: Periapt of Health [5.5e]
item.text:
While wearing this pendant, you can take a Magic action to regain 2d4 + 2 Hit Points. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn. In addition, you have Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Poisoned condition while you wear this pendant.
Source: Dungeon Master's Guide 2024 p. 284

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 43

action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Slashing damage.

action.name: Needles
action.text: Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 30/60 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Piercing damage.

action.text: Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 30/60 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Piercing damage.

note.name: Read
note.text: The floor of this spacious chamber is ten feet lower than that of the three tunnels that branch from it. A horrid stench rises from the floor, where decaying cow carcasses and bovine bones twitch under a network of slowly constricting, otherworldly roots.

note.name: DM Secret!
note.text: Don't reveal the Needle Blights yet. These monsters are hiding. At the start of combat, any character who didn't notice these blights has Disadvantage on their Initiative roll.

note.name: Info
note.text:
The ceilings in this chamber are 20 feet high. The tunnels that lead into this area end abruptly in a 10-foot drop. Along the sunken floor, wicked roots curl around the remains of the ankheg's meals.
Three Needle Blights hide among the roots and carcasses. As a Search action, a character can examine the floor and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check, detecting the blights on a successful check. The blights are Hostile toward the characters. If the characters step on the roots or disturb the carcasses, the blights begin combat.

note.name: Carcasses
note.text:
As a Study action, a character can inspect the dead livestock and make a DC 13 Wisdom (Medicine) check. On a successful check, the character discerns that the cows were violently slain and dragged here by a hulking creature with acidic mandibles.
A character who inspects the carcasses can make a DC 13 Intelligence (Arcana) check. If the check succeeds, the character realizes that this area is an ankheg's nest.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: Amid the carcasses is a burlap sack that contains a loaf of two-day-old bread, three apples, 5 GP, and a Potion of Resistance (Acid). The sack has the initials "R.J." stitched on it.

item.name: Potion of Acid Resistance [5.5e]
item.text:
When you drink this potion, you have Resistance to Acid damage for 1 hour.
Source: Dungeon Master's Guide 2024 p. 289

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 18

trait.name: Tunneler
trait.text: The ankheg can burrow through solid rock at half its Burrow Speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5 (with Advantage if the target is Grappled by the ankheg), reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) Acid damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13).

action.name: Acid Spray (Recharge 6)
action.text: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 12, each creature in a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 14 (4d6) Acid damage. Success: Half damage.

note.name: Read
note.text: Thick fog fills this damp chamber, which smells of freshly dug dirt. Low, chitinous clacking noises echo within the obscuring mist, accompanied by the sounds of shifting earth and clattering rocks.

note.name: DM Secret!
note.text: The ankheg has Tremorsense, which means it has no trouble finding its way through the fog or noticing the characters when they approach.

note.name: Info
note.text: An Ankheg is using its mandibles to slowly widen this chamber. It is Hostile toward all other creatures and starts combat once it detects them.

note.name: Fog
note.text: This tunnel is Heavily Obscured by fog.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 44

action.name: Constricting Vine
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12). Until the grapple ends, the target takes 4 (1d8) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns, and the blight can't make Constricting Vine attacks.

action.name: Entangling Plants (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The blight casts the Entangle spell, using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12).

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 44

action.name: Constricting Vine
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +4, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 12). Until the grapple ends, the target takes 4 (1d8) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of its turns, and the blight can't make Constricting Vine attacks.

action.name: Entangling Plants (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The blight casts the Entangle spell, using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12).

note.name: Read
note.text:
Luminescent particles float throughout this chamber. Bones litter the earthen floor. Slithering roots worm their way over the graveyard and slowly deconstruct a skeleton clad in shredded leather armor.
The particles gather around the dead warrior, twinkling as an echoing, disembodied voice calls to you. "Who goes there? Are you adventurers? I have been stuck here for an awfully long time. Can you free me?"

note.name: DM Secret!
note.text: Don't reveal the Vine Blight Tangler yet. These monsters are part of the Gulthias Tree's roots and manifest only if those roots are attacked.

note.name: Eddy Note
note.text: They go for the treasure every time I run this one. I thought the scarecrow was supposed to be the one who's missing a brain!

note.name: Info
note.text:
The disembodied voice is that of Justine Fobarb, a human adventurer slain long ago. Her spirit is trapped within her entangled remains. Justine doesn't have a Monster Stat Block and can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect. She is initially Indifferent toward the characters, promising to share her story once freed.
The Gulthias tree's evil roots tether Justine's spirit to this area. A character who inspects the roots and succeeds on a DC 12 Intelligence (Religion) check realizes that by destroying the roots, they can set Justine free.

note.name: Destroying the Roots
note.text:
The Gulthias roots have AC 10; HP 15; and Immunity to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Psychic damage. If the roots take damage, read or paraphrase the following text:
"The roots squeal and writhe in pain. "Look out!" cries the disembodied voice. The vines along the tunnel walls begin to peel away and curl into two bipedal figures that rush toward you.
Roll Initiative!"
The two Vine Blight Tanglers fight the characters.
If the roots are destroyed, Justine's spirit passes to the afterlife, but not before warning the characters of the dangers that lurk within these tunnels.

note.name: What Justine Knows
note.text:
What Justine Knows. If the characters free Justine's spirit, her attitude improves to Friendly, and she shares the following information:
Ankheg. A big, acid-spitting bug roams these tunnels. It preys on the cattle above and drags them to its nest (area T4).
Demodogs. The vampire Gunthar had three bloodthirsty hounds. Long after they met their end, they've returned as doglike monsters that guard Gunthar's final resting place (area T9).
Gulthias Tree. A Gulthias tree is an accursed plant that might sprout from the blood-soaked stake of a slain vampire. Justine believes such a tree grows in Gunthar's crypt (area T9) and is the source of the blight that poisons this land. Fire can weaken a Gulthias tree, but the only way to truly destroy this evil is to uproot it completely.
Vampire. The vampire legends are true. Long ago, Justine's adventuring party hunted down Gunthar and drove a stake through his heart. But Justine was slain by one of Gunthar's minions, and her fellow party members abandoned her in their quest for glory. Justine's spirit lingered, vainly hoping they would return; eventually, the roots became her cage.
After Justine shares what she knows, the twinkling motes fade, and silence falls over the tunnel once more.

note.name: Never split the party, kids
note.text: What kind of despicable adventurers would abandon a fellow party member when they needed them most? Poor Justine.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: After destroying the roots, a character can inspect Justine's remains. Around her skeleton's neck hangs a silver emblem of Heironeous, a god of chivalry, honor, and justice. The emblem is shaped like a lightning bolt and is worth 25 GP.

item.name: Emblem (holy symbol) [5.5e]
item.text:
A Holy Symbol takes one of the forms in the Holy Symbol table and is bejeweled or painted to channel divine magic. A Cleric or Paladin can use a Holy Symbol as a Spellcasting Focus. For an Emblem to be an effective Holy Symbol, it must be borne on fabric (such as a tabard or banner) or a Shield.
Source: Player's Handbook 2024 p. 226

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 43

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +3, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Slashing damage.

action.name: Needles
action.text: Ranged Attack Roll: +3, range 30/60 ft. Hit: 6 (2d4 + 1) Piercing damage.

note.name: Read
note.text: The tunnel walls disappear behind a mass of snaking black vines. Two bipedal creatures covered in thorns stand between you and the next bend. The spiny guardians take turns scratching their bristling hides with a rusty rake. Farming tools are scattered across the ground.

note.name: DM Secret!
note.text: Don't reveal the Vine Blight Tangler yet. These monsters are hiding. At the start of combat, any character who didn't notice these blights has Disadvantage on their Initiative roll.

note.name: Info
note.text:
Two Needle Blights use a stolen rake to scratch each other's backs in this tunnel. Two Vine Blight Tanglers hide among the vines along the walls, waiting to ambush any non-Plant creatures that pass through this area. The blights are Hostile toward the characters.
As a Search action, a character can scan the vine-covered walls and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check. On a successful check, the character spots the Vine Blight Tanglers.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: Among the rusty farming tools are a pair of gardening gloves, a hoe, a shovel, and an heirloom ring worth 10 GP.

item.name: Gardening Gloves
item.text: Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

item.name: Hoe
item.text: Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

item.name: Shovel [5.5e]
item.text:
Working for 1 hour, you can use a Shovel to dig a hole that is 5 feet on each side in soil or similar material.
Source: Player's Handbook 2024 p. 228

item.name: Heirloom Ring
item.text: Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

note.name: Read
note.text: You stand before the entrance to a buried crypt. Thick vines cover its crumbling stone doors, and gnarled black roots weave across the chamber floor. Near the doors, the vines wrap around a halfling farmhand. She's out cold.

note.name: Info
note.text:
The adventurers who defeated Gunthar sealed the doors to his crypt (area T9). When the ankheg began burrowing beneath Rillard Family Farm, it demolished the doors, unleashing the Gulthias tree and the blight it brings.
The farmhand is Rilly June, Rillard's granddaughter, whom the characters can easily identify thanks to the sketch her grandfather provided. Rilly June has the Restrained and Unconscious conditions, but she is otherwise unharmed.

note.name: Freeing Rilly June
note.text:
As an action, a character can make a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check to free Rilly June from the vines, doing so on a successful check. Once freed, Rilly June wakes up and introduces herself. She is Friendly toward the characters, and can share the following information:
Tunnel. Rilly June heard digging sounds in the farmhouse basement, where she found a tunnel. She followed the tunnel, hoping it might reveal clues about the blighted crops, but she was ambushed by strange creatures and brought here.
Unusual Tree. Rilly June caught a peek at the crypt (area T9). Somehow, there's a tree growing underground there.
You Have My Pitchfork! Rilly June volunteers to accompany the characters as they confront the source of the blight. Her farm is in peril, so she has a right to help defend it.
A character can try to convince Rilly June to return to the surface by using an Influence action to make a DC 15 Charisma (Persuasion) check. On a successful check, Rilly June begrudgingly returns to the safety of the farmhouse. Otherwise, she accompanies the characters for the rest of this adventure.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club

trait.name: Pack Tactics
trait.text: The demodog has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the demodog's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

trait.text: Welcome to the Hellfire Club
