action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The medusa makes two Claw attacks and one Snake Hair attack, or it makes three Poison Ray attacks.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

action.name: Snake Hair
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage plus 14 (4d6) Poison damage.

action.name: Poison Ray
action.text: Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 150 ft. Hit: 11 (2d8 + 2) Poison damage.

action.name: Petrifying Gaze (Recharge 5-6) (bonus action)
action.text: Constitution Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 30-foot Cone. If the medusa sees its reflection in the Cone, the medusa must make this save. First Failure: The target has the Restrained condition and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if it is still Restrained, ending the effect on itself on a success. Second Failure: The target has the Petrified condition instead of the Restrained condition.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The hound makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) Fire damage.

action.name: Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6)

trait.name: Pack Tactics
trait.text: The hound has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hound's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

action.name: Multiattack

action.name: Bite

action.name: Fire Breath (Recharge 5-6)

note.name: Read
note.text:
A judge with a nest of snakes for hair and wearing crimson robes presides over this infernal court. Two short podiums rest before the judge's bench, an imposing monument of black marble flanked by two snarling hounds; flames drip from the hounds' jaws. A lone imp perches atop one of the podiums. It snickers as you enter.
Nine petrified jurors with frightened expressions fill a juror's box to the north. The judge calls the court to order. "Order in the court!" the judge demands. "The accused have finally decided to join us—late and without representation. How do you plead?"

note.name: Info
note.text:
The judge is Yudith Duboix, a no-nonsense Medusa. Judge Deboix is Hostile toward the characters, who have just entered their own trial. Two Hell Hounds ferociously obey the judge's commands.
The trial proceeds through arraignment, statements, and sentencing, as detailed in the following sections. The characters can either represent themselves or allow the Imp Trickster, an unlicensed attorney, to provide them with bad-faith representation.
The jury is composed of adventurers petrified by Judge Deboix. If the characters try to fight the court instead, they might be similarly turned to stone, in which case Dardew can amend his contract (see "Infernal Contract" earlier in this adventure) to also remove the Petrified condition.

note.name: Arraignment
note.text:
After the characters' plea, Judge Duboix summarizes the charges against them, which might include but aren't limited to the following:
Arson. A character is charged with arson if they've ever set anything on fire, including torches.
Failure to Appear. A character is charged with failure to appear if their player has ever arrived late to a game session.
Murder. A character is charged with murder if they've ever slain a creature in combat.
Thievery. A character is charged with thievery if they've ever stolen property, including gold or items found in a dungeon or taken from the dead.
Trespassing. A character is charged with trespassing if they've ever entered a location, including a dungeon, without permission.
At your discretion, other offenses might be worthy of a charge in this kangaroo court.

note.name: Statements
note.text:
he characters can confer and give a statement in their defense. A character can try to convince Judge Duboix of the party's innocence and make a DC 15 Charisma (Persuasion) check. On a successful check, Judge Duboix finds the party not guilty and dismisses the characters from the court. On a failed check (or if the characters chose to let the imp represent them), Judge Duboix rules against the party and moves to sentencing.
If the party is found not guilty, the challenge is considered complete, and the portal to area H1 reopens.

note.name: Sentencing
note.text:
Judge Duboix's sentencing is harsh and swift. If the party is found guilty, the medusa sentences the characters to 1,000 years in prison. The judge then orders the hell hounds to escort the characters to the infernal prison (area H6) for detainment. If the characters refuse, the hell hounds leap into battle. Judge Duboix defends itself as necessary but otherwise abstains from combat. If both hounds are slain, Judge Duboix surrenders and overturns the previous verdict.
If the characters comply with sentencing and later return to this area, the courtroom is empty, and the portal to area H1 reopens.

note.name: Treasure
note.text:
Characters receive treasure as follows:
Defeating Hell Hounds. Each time a character kills a hound, the audience throws 200 GP into this area.
Defeating Judge Duboix. If Judge Duboix dies, the audience throws 2,000 GP into this area.
Innocent Verdict. If the party is found innocent, the audience throws 100 GP into this area.

item.name: Gold Piece (GP) [5.5e]
item.text:
Characters often find coins on their adventures and can spend those coins in shops, inns, and other businesses. Coins come in different denominations based on the relative worth of their material. The Coin Values table lists coins and how much they're worth relative to the Gold Piece, which is the game's main coin. For example, 100 Copper Pieces are worth 1 Gold Piece. A coin weighs about a third of an ounce, so fifty coins weigh a pound.
Coin | Value in GP Copper Piece (CP) | 1/100 Silver Piece (SP) | 1/10 Electrum Piece (EP) | 1/2 Gold Piece (GP) | 1 Platinum Piece (PP) | 10
Source: Player's Handbook 2024 p. 213

trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 30

trait.name: Treasure
trait.text: Any. The monster's treasure hoard can include monetary treasure and any kinds of magic items.

trait.name: Barbed Hide
trait.text: At the start of each of its turns, the devil deals 5 (1d10) Piercing damage to any creature it is grappling or any creature grappling it.

trait.name: Diabolical Restoration
trait.text: If the devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The devil makes one Claws attack and one Tail attack, or it makes two Hurl Flame attacks.

action.name: Claws
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13) from both claws.

action.name: Tail
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. Hit: 14 (2d10 + 3) Slashing damage.

action.name: Hurl Flame
action.text: Ranged Attack Roll: +5, range 150 ft. Hit: 17 (5d6) Fire damage. If the target is a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried, it starts burning.

trait.text: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

trait.name: Baleful Presence
trait.text: Sources of light in a 60-foot Emanation originating from the spawn flicker wildly. Nonmagical sources of Bright Light in that area instead shed Dim Light.

trait.name: Regeneration
trait.text: The spawn regains 5 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

trait.name: Running Leap
trait.text: With a 10-foot running start, the spawn can Long Jump up to 30 feet.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The spawn makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 10 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Slashing damage.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 13). While Grappled, the target has the Blinded condition.

trait.text: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

trait.name: Size
trait.text: Small or Medium

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Monster Manual 2024 p. 177, Player's Handbook 2024 p. 352

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The imp has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Sting
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) Piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) Poison damage.

action.name: Invisibility
action.text: The imp has the Invisible condition for 1 minute. This effect ends early immediately after the imp makes an attack roll or deals damage, or if the imp has the Incapacitated condition.

action.name: Shape-Shift
action.text: The imp shape-shifts to resemble a rat (Speed 20 ft.), a raven (20 ft., fly 60 ft.), or a spider (20 ft., Climb 20 ft.), or it returns to its true form. Its statistics are the same in each form, except for its Speed. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

note.name: Read
note.text: A devil covered in sharp barbs patrols this open-air cell block. The devil rattles its tail against the bars of the six cages, laughing cruelly at the prisoners. A key hangs around the devil's neck.

note.name: Info
note.text:
The warden of this prison is a masochistic Barbed Devil named Khaos, the Bringer of Pain. Khaos is Hostile toward the characters. When they enter, the devil grunts and points them to the biggest cage (cage 1), attacking any who refuse to enter. Khaos then returns to marching up and down the block.
Khaos enjoys catching escaped prisoners and breaking their spirits and bodies. The devil doesn't confiscate inmates' equipment and pretends not to notice if they try to free themselves or one another. Khaos doesn't pursue runaways beyond this area.

note.name: Cages
note.text:
Khaos wears the key to these six locked cages. As a Utilize action, a character can try to pick a cage's lock using Thieves' Tools, doing so with a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. The check is made with Disadvantage if the character is trying to pick the lock from inside the cage.
Alternatively, a character can try to goad Khaos into unlocking their cage. As an Influence action, a character can taunt the barbed devil and make a DC 13 Charisma (Performance) check. On a successful check, Khaos unlocks the door, enters the cage, and strikes that character to teach them a lesson.
The cage's contents are as follows:
Cage 1 contains only a bench chained to the wall.
Cage 2 contains a Demogorgon Spawn captured on the battlefields of Avernus. The spawn is Hostile toward all other creatures and tries to tear them limb from limb if freed from its cell.
Cage 3 is empty.
Cage 4 contains a human Cultist of Demogorgon who warns any who address her that the "Sibilant Beast" will consume them all. The Hostile cultist fights any who enter her cell.
Cage 5 contains an Imp Trickster playing a harmonica. The imp is Indifferent toward the characters but aids them in combat in this area if freed.
Cage 6 is empty.
If a character escapes their cage or frees one of the other imprisoned creatures, Khaos brays with malevolent laughter and fights the escapee.

note.name: Treasure
note.text:
Characters receive treasure as follows:
Defeating Khaos. If a character kills Khaos, the audience throws 2,000 GP into this area.
Defeating the Demogorgon Spawn. If a character kills the Demogorgon Spawn, the audience throws 150 GP into this area.

trait.name: Source
trait.text: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

trait.name: Baleful Presence
trait.text: Sources of light in a 120-foot Emanation originating from the demodragon flicker wildly. Nonmagical sources of Bright Light in that area instead shed Dim Light.

trait.name: Regeneration
trait.text: The demodragon regains 10 Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The demodragon makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 10 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) Piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) Acid damage.

action.name: Acid Breath (Recharge 5–6)
action.text: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC 13, each creature in a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line. Failure: 22 (4d10) Acid damage. Success: Half damage.

note.name: Read
note.text:
Dardew has tilted the odds in the characters' favor. When they enter this area, each character magically gains the benefits of finishing a Short Rest. Then, read or paraphrase the following text:
A river of molten lava flows through this scorched lair. Crumbling rubble, dried viscera, and cracked bones litter the ground, a barren landscape marred by past battles.
The earth trembles as a massive abomination with a sickly gray hide, razor-sharp claws, and two faceless heads rises from a mound of glittering treasure. It stretches its wide, leathery wings and unfurls its heads to reveal two gaping maws of toothed, fleshy petals. The behemoth bellows two bone-chilling roars.
Roll initiative!

note.name: Info
note.text: The final challenge in the Hellfire Games is the Demodragon, an abomination that has slaughtered scores of contestants. The Demodragon is Hostile toward the characters and fights to the death.

note.name: Lava
note.text: Rivers of lava flow throughout this area, as shown on the map. A creature that enters the lava for the first time on a turn or starts its turn in the lava takes 6d10 fire damage.

note.name: Treasure
note.text: The Demodragon's hoard contains the following: an Adamantine Armor (Breastplate), a Spiked Shield, a Potion of Healing, and 2,420 GP.

item.name: Adamantine Breastplate [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Uncommon
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any Critical Hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Source: Dungeon Master's Guide 2024 p. 227

item.name: Sentinel Shield [5.5e]
item.text:
Rarity: Uncommon (requires Attunement)
Crude metal spikes adorn this magic Shield. It has the following properties that you can use while holding it. Shield Bash. When you take the Attack action, you can make one of the attack rolls using the Shield against a target within 5 feet of yourself. Apply your Proficiency Bonus and Strength modifier to the attack roll. On a hit, the Shield deals Piercing damage equal to 2d6 plus your Strength modifier. Spike Salvo. As a Magic action, you can cause spikes to erupt from the Shield in a 30-foot Cone. Each creature in the Cone makes a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club 2024

item.name: Infernal Contract
item.text:
This CONTRACT is made and entered into by and between the undersigned (hereinafter the "Signee") and Dardew the Banished (hereinafter the "Fiend") on behalf of the diabolical courts of the Nine Hells of Baator. Whereas the Signee intends to receive services rendered by the Fiend, as dictated by the terms of this Contract, which are as follows:
Rendering of Aid. When the Signee enters into this agreement, the Signee shall receive immediate aid in the form of healing magic.
1.1. Aid rendered to the Signee must be commensurate with the total value of the dice roll, as determined by the accompanying Game of Chance (defined in Section 2).
1.2. In lieu of healing magic, a benefit of equal magnitude may be substituted for this aid, as proposed by the Signee or the Fiend before entering into this agreement. Substitutions are subject to approval by both parties.
Game of Chance. When the Signee enters into this agreement, the Signee must roll three (3) or more six-sided dice.
2.1. The Signee may choose to roll more than three (3) dice to increase the magnitude of aid received, as outlined in Section 1.1.
2.2. The Signee must declare the number of dice before rolling.
2.3. If the Signee rolls a six (6) on three (3) or more of these dice, the Signee forfeits their soul, as outlined in Section 3, and the Signee can't benefit from this contract again.
Soul Forfeiture. In the instance of three (3) or more sixes (6) being rolled during the Game of Chance (see Section 2), the Signee forfeits their soul.
3.1. The Signee retains their soul for the remainder of the Signee's mortal life.
3.2. Upon the Signee's death, the Signee's soul becomes the property of the Fiend. Resurrection does not return a forfeited soul to the Signee.
Renewal. A Signee who has not already forfeited their soul may verbally renew this agreement, entering into it again and entitling the Signee to additional aid.
Nullification. The Fiend, an archdevil, or any devil designated by the Court Infernal or the Fiend may void this Contract at any time, returning all forfeited souls to their previous owners.
5.1. Destruction of this Contract or the Fiend does not void its terms.
I, the Signee, hereby acknowledge this contract as legitimate and agree to uphold its terms.
Signed,
Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club

item.name: Hellfire Club Pendant
item.text:
The pendant depicts the face of a red devil flanked by a flail and flaming sword.
Source: Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club

note.name: Adventure Info
note.text:
Devil, Metal, Die! is an adventure for Level 3 Characters.
This adventure is designed to fill one or two sessions of play.
If you aren't using the Map of Greyhawkins, it can begin in any tavern before descending into the Nine Hells.

note.name: Key Plot Points
note.text:
This information is key to the adventure's story:
Plot Point 1. Dardew invites the characters to compete in the Hellfire Games. If they accept, they're transported to the Hellfire Dome.
Plot Point 2. In the Hellfire Dome, the characters face off against devils, demons, and a dragon unlike any other.
Plot Point 3. Spectators shower the characters with gold throughout the competition. If a character falls in battle, Dardew intercedes and offers them the chance to rise and fight again as part of an infernal pact.
Plot Point 4. The characters either complete all the challenges or die trying. Any survivors are inducted into the Hellfire Club.

note.name: DM Preparation
note.text:
Before running the adventure, prepare as follows:
Step 1. Read the "Adventure Background" and "Deal with the Devil" sections.
Step 2. Familiarize yourself with the Infernal Contract Handout and the Hellfire Dome map.
Step 3. Familiarize yourself with the creatures in this adventure, which are as follows:
1 Barbed Devil 1 Bearded Devil 5 Cultists of Demogorgon Dardew 5 Demobats 1 Demodragon 1 Demogorgon Spawn 6 Dretches 3 Gnoll Warriors 2 Hell Hounds 1 Imp Trickster 1 Medusa
Step 4. Familiarize yourself with the Magic Items in this adventure, which are as follows:
Adamantine Armor (Breastplate) Potion of Healing Spiked Shield

note.name: Eddy Note
note.text:
This is it: the final trial to see who's worthy of joining Hellfire! This deadly gauntlet separates the wheat from the chaff, real heroes from would-be plunderers. Make sure everyone can make it to game night. If they skip this one, they're OUT. Lure them with the promise of epic adventure, priceless treasures, and CERTAIN DEATH.
Oh, and snacks. Bring those, too.

note.name: Player Assistance
note.text:
You can simplify the DM's role by having the a player handle tasks typically assigned to the DM. Before play begins, see if a player would like to be the Rules Consultant. That player references the D&D Beyond Basic Rules as needed.
Monster Wranglers. Whenever the characters encounter Hostile monsters, one or two players can run those monsters using the referenced stat blocks. (If you have two players doing this, they can take turns.)
Rules Consultant. One player references the D&D Beyond Basic Rules as needed.

note.name: Adventure Background
note.text:
From the fires of the Nine Hells rises the infamous Hellfire Dome, a diabolical arena where contestants of ill repute vie for gold and glory. Death, betrayal, and priceless treasures await those who endure the lethal games in the dome, which are spectated by an audience of disorderly fiends.
Contestants must rely on strength and wits to overcome the diabolical gauntlet that awaits them in the dome. The last and greatest of these trials is a showdown with the Demodragon: a wretched, two-headed terror created by Demogorgon, the Prince of Demons. Those who slay the Demodragon depart the Nine Hells as wealthy champions.
Winners are also inducted into the Hellfire Club, an exclusive association of infernal origin respected by devils of every rank. Members of the Hellfire Club have a reputation for being tough, fearless, and above all—totally metal.
Every six years, the Hellfire Dome opens its doors to non-Fiends, allowing them to compete in a series of infernal challenges. Dardew the Banished, a cambion with aspirations of grandeur, invites the characters to compete in the arena. If victorious, the characters will be crowned members of the legendary Hellfire Club, and Dardew will rise in the fiery ranks. However, if the characters lose, they might forfeit their souls.

note.name: Dardew the Banished
note.text: This year's master of ceremonies for the Hellfire Games is a music-loving fiend named Dardew the Banished. A lowly cambion, Dardew hopes to ascend the ranks of the Nine Hells through panache and showmanship, impressing powerful devils in the audience with an unforgettable performance. He believes the characters are his ticket to fame.

note.name: Deal with the Devil
note.text:
Few survive the Hellfire Dome in one piece, but those who make deals with devils have a better chance at making it out alive. Early deaths among contestants can make for an underwhelming display, and Dardew can't afford for this year's Hellfire Games to disappoint.
To give the characters an edge in the arena, Dardew has arranged a special contract to keep them fighting beyond the brink of death. Still, no fiend can resist corrupting a mortal soul, especially one that belongs to a good-hearted adventurer. The characters would be wise to consider the contract's fine print before they sign.

note.name: Souls for sale!
note.text: Death is a part of the game, but it doesn't have to be the end. And in this adventure, it can be fun for everyone involved! Celebrate players who push their luck and amplify this great, bloody, gladiatorial spectacle. You can always give them their characters' souls back later... if you're a pushover.

note.name: Infernal Contract
note.text:
Whenever a character drops to 0 Hit Points in the Hellfire Dome, Dardew extends an infernal contract to that character. This event takes place outside the Initiative order, which resumes after the character accepts or declines Dardew's offer.
Read or paraphrase the following text:
"The scene around you freezes as Dardew appears and unfurls a lengthy contract. "I've got a good feeling about you. How would you like to keep going?" "
Show that player the Infernal Contract Handout and ask them if their character agrees to the following terms:
●Defying Death. When you drop to 0 Hit Points while in the Hellfire Dome—including when you are first offered this contract—you can roll any number of d6s (minimum of 3d6) and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll's total (no action required). If you roll a 6 on three or more of these dice, you also forfeit your soul and can't benefit from the contract in this way again.
●Forfeiting Your Soul. If you forfeit your soul, it becomes the property of Dardew the Banished on your death.
●Voiding the Contract. As a Magic action, Dardew or an archdevil can void this contract, returning any forfeited souls to their previous owners. Destroying the contract or its arbiter doesn't void the agreement.
If the character accepts these terms, they sign the contract. Dardew then collects the contract and vanishes in a cloud of brimstone. Dardew reappears to offer the same contract to any character who drops to 0 Hit Points while in the Hellfire Dome, even if the character previously rejected the contract.

note.name: This is Hellfire
note.text:
If the characters reject Dardew, your players are either scaredy cats or a bunch of *****. Either way, put on your favorite mix and wait 'em out. They'll come to their senses eventually.
It's D&D or bust.

note.name: Beginning the Adventure
note.text:
When the players are ready to begin the adventure, read or paraphrase the following text:
"You sit in an unremarkable tavern somewhere in Greyhawkins when, in a burst of brimstone, a winged, red-skinned fiend with a mane of curly hair and a boyish smile appears. He lands atop one of the tables with an exaggerated bow.
"Greetings, mortal adventurers. My name is Dardew. I hope I'm not interrupting your meal," he says coyly.
"I've come to invite you to compete in the Hellfire Games. I've been watching you for some time now, and I believe you just might have what it takes to join the esteemed Hellfire Club. Care to play the devils' game?" "
Dardew has come to recruit the characters for the Hellfire Games. He is Indifferent toward the party—but still charming—and shares the following information about the competition:
Hellfire Games. The Hellfire Games are a sexennial competition in which contestants battle for prestige, treasure, and induction into the exclusive Hellfire Club.
Hellfire Club. The Hellfire Club is an invite-only club whose members include epic heroes, high-ranking devils, and figures of legend.
Hellfire Dome. The games are held in the Hellfire Dome, an arena far from Greyhawkins; Dardew is this year's host and can take the characters there.
If the characters ask about the risk, Dardew explains that while the Hellfire Games are dangerous, the characters have nothing to worry about. He has prepared a special contract to help the characters cheat death (see "Deal with the Devil"), though he doesn't delve into specifics.

note.name: Accepting the Invitation
note.text:
If the characters accept Dardew's invitation, he magically transports them to the Hellfire Dome. Read or paraphrase the following text:
"That's the spirit!" Dardew laughs and claps his hands, and a cloud of red smoke engulfs you.
The characters begin the games in area H1 of the Hellfire Dome.

note.name: Declining the Invitation
note.text:
If the characters decline, read or paraphrase:
"Hmm, not what I expected. Oh, well! Maybe next time." Dardew shrugs and disappears with a puff of smoke.
At your discretion, Dardew might return later to reissue his invitation, perhaps offering an important insight or piece of information in return for the characters' participation.

note.name: Hellfire Dome
note.text:
Devils prize competition, treachery, and power, so they created the Hellfire Dome, a gauntlet of deadly scenarios designed to feature all three. These challenges take place in a volcanic pit on Avernus, the first layer of the Nine Hells. Just outside the arid dome lies an endless wasteland razed in the Blood War, an eternal conflict between devils and demons.
The Hellfire Dome is a multistory arena hewn from obsidian. A massive dome forged from infernal steel covers the arena, shielding it from meteors that fall from Avernus's skies. Beneath the dome, an infernal steel cage prevents contestants from escaping.
Thousands of devils gather to watch the Hellfire Games. These rowdy onlookers sometimes cast coins into the pit, showering contenders with gold. Unpopular contestants occasionally receive a ball of flame hurled by a disgruntled fiend.
Refer to the Hellfire Dome map and note that the players begin in the center of area H1. Don't reveal the rest of the map's contents yet, as it is not not initially visible to the characters.

note.name: Hellfire Dome Features
note.text:
The Hellfire Dome has the following features:
Ambiance. The arena is dry and oppressively hot, and it reeks of blood and brimstone. The audience roars with cheers and howls.
Ceilings. Aside from the dome over the arena, the Hellfire Dome has no ceilings. An indestructible cage 60 feet above the pit separates contestants from the audience.
Lighting. Unless otherwise specified, areas in the dome are in Bright Light.
Portals. Each of the lettered portals on Hellfire Dome map is a gateway of harmless fire linked to another area in the arena. A creature that enters a portal emerges within 5 feet of the portal with the matching letter on Hellfire Dome map or in the nearest unoccupied space.

note.name: Conclusion
note.text:
The Hellfire Games conclude when the characters vanquish the Demodragon or die trying. If the characters defeat the Demodragon, read or paraphrase the following text:
"With an earthshaking crash, the Demodragon collapses dead before you. The crowd goes wild.
Dardew soars overhead. "Wow! What a show!" he crows. "Friends and fiends, please join me in welcoming our latest Hellfire champions!"
He flies toward you and says quietly, "Adventurers, now would be a great time to take a bow. You've earned it." "
In addition to the treasure they collected in the Hellfire Dome, the characters earn the following rewards for winning the Hellfire Games:
Hellfire Club Membership. Dardew invites characters to join the Hellfire Club. If they accept his invitation, they each receive a pendant worth 50 GP. The pendant depicts the face of a red devil flanked by a flail and flaming sword.
Hellfire Hall of Fame. The characters are inducted into the Hellfire Hall of Fame. Their names are recorded forever in the annals of infernal history alongside other legendary heroes and villains.
Voiding the Infernal Contract. If any character entered into an infernal contract with Dardew (see "Infernal Contract" earlier in this adventure), Dardew voids the contract.
When the characters are ready to leave the Nine Hells, Dardew magically returns them to Greyhawkins. Before he departs, Dardew thanks them for the magnificent performance.

note.name: Eddy Note
note.text:
Congratulations.
Hellfire is officially in your hands.
You should take a bow, too. Being a DM can feel overwhelming sometimes, but know this: You're not alone. I promise. If you ever need a pep talk, I'm just a phone call away. Save me a seat at your table. I'll be back to see what you've done with the place.

note.name: Adventure File
note.text:
The File contains all the Information and Encounters found in Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club.
If you want to use different players than those included in the adventure, it is recommended to edit the existing names rather than add or delete them.
New players need to be manually added to the existing encounters and deleting existing players will remove them from all encounters in the file.

note.name: Introduction
note.text: Dungeons & Dragons is a cooperative game in which players create exciting fantasy stories guided by a Dungeon Master, or DM. Imagination and chance determine the fate of the players' characters.

note.name: An Ongoing Game
note.text: Each time you gather to play D&D, you're playing a single game session. These sessions connect to form adventures like the ones in this product. The next time you play, the story picks up where you left off.

note.name: Choose Your Role
note.text: To play D&D with Stranger Things: Welcome to the Hellfire Club, you need two to five people to play as characters and one person to be the Dungeon Master.

note.name: Playing a Character
note.text: If you want to be one of the protagonists in your group's adventures, consider playing a character. Your character is your alter ego in the fantasy world of the game. Here's what these players do.

note.name: Choose a Character
note.text:
Select from the following five characters from the world of Stranger Things:
● Lady Applejack. Played by Erica Sinclair, Lady Applejack is a Chaotic Good, Elf Rogue who will sneak behind any monster, stab them in the back with her kukri, and smile as she watches them die a slow, agonizing death. ● Nog the Dwarf. Dustin Henderson's sturdy Cleric, Nog, has gotten his companions out of a bind more times than he can count. Nog is a devotee of Heironeous, a god of chivalry, justice, and war. ● Sundar the Bold. A gallant and unyielding warrior, Sundar the Bold bravely defends his companions—just like this Fighter's player, Lucas Sinclair. ● Tayr the Paladin. When Mike Wheeler isn't behind the DM screen in his parents' basement, he dusts off his trusty Paladin, Tayr. The heart of the party, Tayr unites his companions and leads the charge in their fight against evil. ● Will the Wise. Will Byer's Wizard is a mainstay of the party and is always up for another adventure. When Will the Wise casts a spell, his magic sometimes manifests in unexpected colors.

note.name: Team Up
note.text: Your character joins the other players' characters to form an adventuring party, often referred to as "the party." These adventurers are allies who face challenges and fantastical situations together. Each character has distinctive capabilities that complement those of the other characters.

note.name: Venture Forth
note.text: Your character's group explores locations and events presented by the DM. You can respond to them however you can imagine, guided by the rules in the D&D Beyond Basic Rules. Although the DM controls all the monsters you encounter, the DM isn't your adversary. The DM guides your party's journey as your characters become more powerful.

note.name: Being the Dungeon Master
note.text:
If you want to be the mastermind of the game, consider being the DM. Here's what DMs do:
Run the Adventure. You run one or more of the adventures.
Guide the Story. You narrate much of the action during play, describing locations and creatures that the adventurers face. The players decide what their characters do as they navigate hazards and choose what to explore. Then you use a combination of imagination and the game's rules to determine the results of the adventurers' decisions.
Adjudicate the Rules. You oversee how the group uses the game's rules, making sure the rules serve the group's fun. You'll want to read the D&D Beyond Basic Rules, and you'll find the rules glossary essential.

note.name: Eddy Note
note.text: Don't worry, Henderson. I'm not going to leave you high and dry. Papa Bear is here. I've left notes like this throughout the adventures to give you a peek behind the scenes and help you run these adventures, but you gotta make 'em your own. You can do this. I believe in you.

note.name: Swapping Roles
note.text:
Playing a character and being the Dungeon Master aren't permanent roles. Each adventure is a self-contained story. Whenever you begin an adventure, determine who will be the DM for that story.
If your group consists of five or fewer players, the previous DM can either choose from the remaining characters or play as the new DM's old character. For groups of six, the previous DM always inherits the available character. Either way, ensure that the character's level is appropriate for the upcoming adventure. The adventures span levels 1–3.

note.name: Getting Started
note.text: Once you've chosen the DM, follow the instructions below to begin play.

note.name: If You're Playing a Character
note.text:
Follow these steps to prepare your character:
● Choose a Character. Pick one of the five level 1 Characters from appendix D. ● Review Your Character. Take a moment to look over your Character Sheet. When you're ready, introduce your character to the other players.

note.name: If You're the Dungeon Master
note.text:
Follow these steps if you're the DM:
● Review the D&D Beyond Basic Rules. Familiarize yourself with the D&D Beyond Basic Rules. You don't need to memorize every rule before you play, but you should understand the rhythm of play and where to find relevant information when needed. ● Prepare the First Adventure. "The Vanishing Gnome" is designed for level 1 characters. Read the Key Plot Points and Adventure Background sections. These sections present important context for the adventure, though the story that ultimately emerges is the one your group experiences.
Now you're ready to play D&D. Hell yeah!

note.name: Running the Adventures
note.text:
The following information is key to running the adventures.
Text that appears in a "Read Note" is meant to be read aloud or paraphrased for the players when their characters first arrive at a location or under a specific circumstance, as described in the text.
When a monster's name appears in Bold type, that's a visual cue to say, "Hey, DM, you should get this stat block ready. You're going to need it."
Magic items are described in Appendix A, while NPCs and Monsters are described in Appendix B.
